title: Resilient Automation API Port Binding
description: Fix automation server startup so local development always gets an available port instead of failing with "Address already in use: bind". Add deterministic port selection/fallback, expose the final bound port, and keep automation usable across parallel runs.
definition-of-done: 1. Automation runtime no longer hard-fails when the default port is occupied. 2. Runtime attempts configured port first and falls back to an open port in a bounded range (or ephemeral). 3. Final bound port is logged clearly and available via automation health endpoint. 4. Optional override supports fixed port for deterministic tests (`ixdar.automation.port`). 5. Clear error only when no ports are available after retry strategy. 6. Unit/integration test covers occupied-port fallback behavior.
testing-plan: 1. Launch one app instance with automation enabled on default port and verify health endpoint responds. 2. Launch a second instance while first is running and verify second instance binds a different open port automatically. 3. Verify `/health` reports actual bound port for each instance. 4. Force invalid/blocked configuration and verify actionable error messaging. 5. Regression test recording/replay endpoints on fallback-bound port.
todos: Add port binding strategy in `AutomationRuntime` (configured port -> fallback range -> optional ephemeral)
Update `AutomationApiServer`/runtime startup contract to return actual bound port
Log selected port and include in health payload consistently
Add helper utility for probing available loopback ports
Add tests for occupied-port fallback and deterministic override behavior
Document automation port behavior in developer docs
unknowns: Preferred fallback strategy: fixed range vs ephemeral port
Whether CLI tooling assumes static default port and needs discovery support
Whether parallel CI agents require explicit port reservation
